commit.message:
Retire sous-titre bornes sur page location-photobooth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace ironclaw with nullclaw (#2)

* Replace ironclaw with nullclaw across the fleet

Nullclaw is a minimal AI assistant infrastructure written in Zig that
replaces ironclaw. Uses SQLite-based hybrid memory instead of PostgreSQL,
making the module significantly simpler. Removes the workstation-only
PostgreSQL setup, identity-sync activation scripts, and DB ownership
services that ironclaw required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace qwen3:14b with Qwen3.5-9B Q6_K sideloaded from HuggingFace

Adds a systemd oneshot service that downloads the Q6_K GGUF from
bartowski on HuggingFace, imports it into ollama via Modelfile, then
cleans up the GGUF. Skips if the model already exists. Sets
OLLAMA_MODEL env var to the new model name for nullclaw.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add OpenSandbox server for nullclaw sandboxed execution

Packages opensandbox-server (Alibaba's AI sandbox control plane) from
source and adds it to the workstation as a systemd service. Enables
Docker runtime, pre-pulls nullclaw + execd + egress container images,
and exposes the API on port 8080 over Tailscale.

This allows running nullclaw agents in isolated Docker containers with
network policy enforcement (egress filtering) and filesystem sandboxing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

update burp-authentication-mapper: token tracking, auth filtering, security assessment, and pre-flight check

- Add configurable RESPONSE_TOKEN_FIELDS and AUTH_REQUEST_HEADERS registries for extensible token detection
- Extract auth tokens from JSON response bodies and custom request headers (X-Token, Authorization, etc.)
- Track full token lifecycle: issuance endpoint, request count, headers used; orphaned-token fallback for tokens not in captured responses
- Filter report: skip hosts with no auth identifiers; show only auth-annotated steps in sequence diagrams
- Add Token Identifiers section per host alongside Session Identifiers and CSRF sections
- Classify token-based auth flows as distinct authentication type
- Add Burp MCP connectivity pre-flight check before fetching live history
- Add Step 4 Security Assessment: evaluates captured flow against security_checklist.md across 8 categories with Pass/Fail/Cannot determine ratings
- Update README, auth_patterns.md, and example_output.md to reflect all changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 2 fix: merge left+right hands before tokenization

REMI is a single-track format. Previously, split_files_for_training
processed each MIDI track independently, producing separate right-hand
and left-hand chunks. The model fine-tuned on those learned single-hand
patterns and generated single-hand output.

Fix: merge all tracks into one before chunking and seeding.
- Added merge_tracks() utility in setup cell
- All 19 corpus MIDIs now produce two-hand chunks (58 train, 5 valid)
- Lost waltz seed is now 1 merged sequence (1148 tokens, was 2x separate)
- Reduced pitch augmentation from ±12 to ±3/±5 semitones
- Added data/chunked/ and data/merged/ to .gitignore (regenerable)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: entrypoint hangs on docker stop — rewrite shutdown logic (#282)

`docker compose stop` hangs indefinitely because `wait` on the PM2
child PID doesn't get interrupted by SIGTERM reliably (PM2 catches
the signal itself).

Fix:
- Replace `wait` with a sleep loop that bash can interrupt on signals
- Add direct `kill` of PM2 PID as fallback after `pm2 kill`
- Add explicit `exit 0` in cleanup handler
- Guard against double cleanup with SHUTTING_DOWN flag

Co-authored-by: zylos-01 <zylos-01@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Use npm install instead of npm ci in node Dockerfile

npm ci requires a package-lock.json which doesn't exist in docker/node/.

ci: install golangci-lint via go install to match project Go version

Pre-built golangci-lint binaries are compiled with go1.24, but
go.mod requires go1.25+. Installing via go install compiles the
linter with the project's own toolchain, eliminating the version
mismatch. Also runs go mod tidy which bumped go directive to 1.25.0
(required by a transitive dependency).

commit.message:
perf: fix SVG rasterisation scale — rasterise at ¼ size then upscale bitmap

Previous attempt used .resize(w, h) on SVG input which passes the target
dimensions directly to librsvg, so it still rendered feMorphology at full
25MP. Fix: call .toBuffer() first to rasterise at the SVG's declared ¼
size (~1.6MP), then resize the resulting PNG bitmap with Lanczos.

feMorphology at ¼ scale (~1.6MP) should take ~3s vs ~55s at full size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add install-deps.sh script for build-from-source quick start

The README quick start assumed clang-14, libbpf, bpftool, and Go were
already installed. On a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 instance, `make` fails with
missing headers (vmlinux.h, bpf_helpers.h) and tools (llvm-strip-14,
staticcheck). This script installs exactly what's needed and verifies
each dependency before handing off to make.

Tested end-to-end on a fresh AWS g4dn.xlarge (Ubuntu 24.04 Deep
Learning AMI) — all 23 test packages pass, staticcheck clean.

commit.message:
Fix maintain-benefits quality criteria and remove dead log step (#46)

* Replace check-links.yml with maintain-benefits workflow

Consolidates weekly link health checking and a new quality review pass
into a single Sunday workflow. maintain-benefits checks every existing
entry for broken/redirected links and flags entries that fail the
quality bar (specificity, self-serve, direct signup link, offer type
accuracy). Produces one consolidated issue instead of separate link and
quality reports.

Removes check-links.yml. Updates CLAUDE.md workflow table and response
instructions. Updates agent/index.html schedule and output descriptions
to reflect the new workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make maintain-benefits quality criteria mechanical and remove dead log step

Quality criteria in Step 3 now rely on URL structure and description
text rather than LLM page-content judgment, reducing false positives.
Each criterion specifies exactly what to check and instructs the agent
to skip uncertain cases. Removes Step 5 (log file write), which was
silently discarded on every run because the workflow has no
create-pull-request capability to persist file changes.

Also removes the unused edit tool from the frontmatter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
回測引擎新增 20+ 專業量化指標（Sortino, Calmar, VaR, Hurst 等）

在 runBacktest() 加入完整機構級量化指標計算：
- 風險調整報酬：Sortino, Calmar, Omega
- 風險指標：VaR 95%/99%, CVaR, 年化波動率
- 統計特徵：Skewness, Kurtosis, Hurst Exponent, Tail Ratio
- 交易品質：Payoff Ratio, EV, Recovery Factor
- 持倉分析：最大連勝/連敗, 平均持有天數
- 痛苦指標：Ulcer Index, Pain Ratio

回測結果 UI 升級為分類儀表板（6 類 20+ 指標）

tracked via DB outcomes (signal_model='sniper'). Max3 vs Max2 sizing
evaluated offline via sleeve simulator once live trades accumulate.

commit.message:
Fix natural-mode crop to output at native bounding-box dimensions

Previously, natural mode scaled the cropped region back up to 1024px wide,
making the output the same size as the source — no visible crop. Now the
output canvas is exactly srcW × srcH (the tight bounding box of non-transparent
pixels), producing a genuinely smaller image when the object doesn't fill the
full source canvas.

Square mode is unchanged: content is letterboxed inside canvasWidth × canvasWidth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

benchmark: add verified_refinement variant with fail-first Hunter mock

Add _FailFirstHunterLLM that reverses ranking on its first score call,
forcing the orchestrator refinement loop to recover. New variant
verified_refinement exercises the Architect->Hunter feedback path that
the standard verified variant cannot test with deterministic mocks.
